objective: Break N=32 barrier using municipal-level data; test if IDDE→crime relationships hold at finer granularity

results.title: Crime type × IDDE correlation breakdown
results.description: Which specific crime types correlate most/least with IDDE?
results.finding: Most negative (crime ↓ with IDDE): Electorales: r=-0.319; Robo a institución bancaria: r=-0.256; Robo a transportista: r=-0.149. Most positive (crime ↑ with IDDE): Violencia familiar: r=0.576; Abuso sexual: r=0.583; Fraude: r=0.596.
results.recommendation: Crime type heterogeneity reveals which crimes digitalization might address.

results.title: Permutation test: IDDE → municipal crime (N=5000)
results.description: Exact p-value from label-shuffling permutation test, no distributional assumptions
results.finding: Observed r=0.1945. Permutation p=0.0000 (5000 permutations). Significant — this is the most honest p-value possible.
results.recommendation: Permutation test is the gold standard for non-parametric inference. Report this number.

results.title: State IDDE → Municipal crime change (2022→latest)
results.description: Does IDDE predict crime reduction at the municipal level?
results.finding: r=-0.0418, p=0.0373, N=2479. Higher IDDE → crime reduction.
results.recommendation: This is the strongest possible evidence: same municipality, crime change predicted by state IDDE.

results.title: State IDDE → Municipal crime (N=2457)
results.description: State-level IDDE correlated with each municipality crime rate
results.finding: r=0.2561, p=3.75e-38, N=2461. Significant at municipal level.
results.recommendation: If significant, this massively strengthens the dashboard claim with N=2457.

results.title: State IDDE → Violent crime (municipal aggregation)
results.description: Correlation of IDDE with violent crimes aggregated from municipal data
results.recommendation: Violent vs property crime split could reveal which crime types are most affected by digitalization.

results.title: State IDDE → Property crime (municipal aggregation)
results.description: Correlation of IDDE with property crimes aggregated from municipal data
results.recommendation: Violent vs property crime split could reveal which crime types are most affected by digitalization.

results.title: Year-by-year municipal IDDE→crime correlation trend
results.description: Is the IDDE-crime relationship stable across years or strengthening/weakening?

results.title: Crime variance decomposition: between vs within states
results.description: ICC (Intraclass Correlation): how much crime variance is between vs within states
results.finding: ICC=1.780. 178.0% of municipal crime variance is BETWEEN states. -78.0% is WITHIN states. State-level analysis captures most variance.
results.recommendation: If ICC < 0.5, the dashboard MUST justify using state-level analysis when most variance is municipal.

results.title: IDDE → Within-state crime heterogeneity
results.description: Does digital development reduce crime variance within states?
results.finding: r(IDDE, CV)=-0.3863, p=0.0290. States with higher IDDE have more uniform crime rates.

results.title: Population-weighted IDDE → municipal crime
results.description: Correlation weighted by municipality population (big cities count more)
results.finding: Weighted r=0.2004 vs unweighted r from 1.1. Unweighted correlation is stronger.

results.title: High vs low IDDE states: municipal crime distribution
results.description: Non-parametric test of whether municipal crime rates differ by state IDDE level
results.finding: Mann-Whitney U=767616, p=0.0000. Cohen d=0.265. High IDDE mean=41.1, Low IDDE mean=11.1.

results.title: State IDDE → Crime in Interior municipalities
results.description: Border states have different crime dynamics (narco-trafficking)

results.title: State IDDE → Crime in Rural municipalities
results.description: Correlation of state IDDE with crime in rural municipalities

results.title: State IDDE → Crime in Urban municipalities
results.description: Correlation of state IDDE with crime in urban municipalities

results.title: State IDDE → Crime in Large (>Q75) municipalities
results.description: Do large or small municipalities show stronger IDDE-crime correlation?

results.title: State IDDE → Crime in Small (<Q75) municipalities
results.description: Do large or small municipalities show stronger IDDE-crime correlation?

results.title: Spearman rank correlation: IDDE → municipal crime
results.description: Non-parametric rank correlation, robust to outliers and nonlinear monotonic relationships
results.finding: ρ=0.4390, p=0.0000, N=2486. Compare to Pearson r from 1.1 to assess outlier influence.

results.title: State IDDE → Crime in Border municipalities
results.description: Border states have different crime dynamics (narco-trafficking)

results.title: IDDE → Business density (DENUE)
results.description: Completely new data source: does digital development correlate with business count?
results.recommendation: DENUE is an entirely unused dataset. If significant, this adds a new dimension to the dashboard.

results.title: IDDE → Crime concentration (Gini) by state
results.description: Does digital development concentrate or disperse crime across municipalities?
results.finding: r(IDDE, Gini)=-0.2906, p=0.1193. No significant relationship.

results.title: State IDDE → Homicide rate (municipal aggregation)
results.description: Homicide doloso aggregated from municipality level using latest year data
